 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.3","title":"Prompt pack: Role-specific agents output structured deltas","description":"# Goal
Define role prompts for a multi-agent team so outputs are *mergeable*, not essays.

Target roles (initial):
- **Formalizer (Codex/GPT)**: turn narratives into explicit hypotheses/tests + scoring.
- **Architect/Critic (Opus)**: enforce operator algebra + guardrails + conceptual coherence.
- **Novelty/Alt-cluster (Gemini)**: generate "third alternatives" and reframings.

# Key design constraint
Each role must emit:
- a structured artifact delta format (e.g., fenced JSON or strict markdown sections)
- citations to transcript anchors where relevant

# Acceptance criteria
- Prompts are written for each role.
- Each prompt specifies output schema (delta format) and strict sections.
- Prompts explicitly require third alternative + potency checks.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:22:32.393341617-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:14:14.219133334-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:14:14.219133334-05:00","close_reason":"All P1 children complete","labels":["prompts","roles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:22:32.394277278-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.3.1","title":"Choose and specify the structured delta output format","description":"Decide how agents will express updates:
- strict markdown sections, OR
- fenced JSON (preferred for parsing), OR
- JSON Patch-like operations.

Done when: format is chosen and specified with examples.

Done when: each prompt is copy/paste ready.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4","title":"Protocol: Agent Mail thread contract (message types + ack semantics)","description":"# Goal
Standardize how sessions use Agent Mail so orchestration is deterministic.

This is critical in a **no vendor API** architecture because sessions are *messages + compilation*:
- agents run in terminal cockpits (often via `ntm`)
- they post results back to Agent Mail threads

So: we need a contract the compiler can rely on.

---

# Define
- thread id conventions (what counts as a session thread)
- message types (kickoff, role response/delta, compiled artifact, critique/round-2)
- subject conventions
- required body sections
- ack policy (when required, how to track)

---

# Acceptance
- A written spec exists for message types + required fields.
- The CLI and web can rely on these conventions.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:22:32.425227128-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:53:53.192825848-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:53:53.192825848-05:00","close_reason":"All child specs completed: thread_subject_conventions_v0.1.md, message_body_schema_v0.1.md, agent_mail_contracts_v0.1.md. CLI and web can rely on these conventions.","labels":["agent-mail","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:22:32.426082859-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1","title":"Specify thread + subject conventions","description":"# Goal
Define conventions for:
- thread IDs (session IDs)
- message subjects (prefixing, role tags)

This is the ergonomic layer that makes a mailbox/thread list intelligible at a glance.

---

# Thread ID convention (recommended)
- Engineering work: use the Beads ID as the Agent Mail `thread_id`.
- Research sessions: use `RS-YYYYMMDD-short-slug` (stable, human-readable).

---

# Subject convention (recommended)
Use short, machine-detectable prefixes:
- KICKOFF: …
- DELTA[role]: …  (e.g., DELTA[gpt], DELTA[opus], DELTA[gemini])
- COMPILED: …
- CRITIQUE: …

---

# Acceptance
- A human can look at an inbox and understand what happened in a thread instantly.
- An orchestrator can classify messages without brittle heuristics.

Includes validation rules and cross-reference contract.","labels":["agent-mail","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:23:10.91673657-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Done when: orchestration can be deterministic and machine-checked.

Includes ACK semantics table and 12 machine-checkable validation rules.","labels":["agent-mail","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:23:10.948444366-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:33:40.673098414-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.3","title":"Memory: feedback loop (session artifacts → durable memory)","description":"# Goal
Over time, artifacts should feed back into memory so we compound.

This is a later-stage feature; design carefully to avoid garbage-in.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.4","title":"Memory: operational workflow (cm playbook + outcomes)","description":"# Goal
Define how we actually *use* cass-memory day-to-day in this repo:
- how the playbook is seeded / curated
- when to run `cm context` and what to inject
- how to record outcomes (`cm outcome ...`)
- how to avoid memory pollution and over-conditioning

---

# Acceptance
- Operators have a concrete checklist for using cm in a session.
- We have a plan for seeding initial rules without vendor APIs.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:07:45.386313425-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:33:33.423232575-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:33:33.423232575-05:00","close_reason":"Completed runbook: cm categories + context injection + feedback marking workflow","labels":["cass","memory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:07:45.416664938-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.4.1","title":"Seed initial cm playbook categories for Brenner Loop","description":"# Goal
Define an initial taxonomy of cm playbook categories that match Brenner Bot workflows.

Suggested categories:
- protocol-kernel
- prompt-hygiene
- evidence-per-week
- thread-operations
- artifact-compilation
- safety-and-gating

# Acceptance
- A human can add the first handful of rules without guesswork.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.6.4.2","title":"Write operator checklist for cm context injection","description":"# Goal
Define the practical checklist for using memory in a session:
- when to run `cm context`
- recommended defaults (`--no-history`, small `--limit`)
- where to insert in prompt (dedicated MEMORY CONTEXT section)
- provenance labeling + anti-pattern warnings

# Acceptance
- Runbook contains a copy/paste snippet for memory injection.
